title: Suns end Warriors' season, await No. 1 Thunder
content: The Suns knocked out the Warriors 111-96 to secure the No. 8 seed and will face the top-seeded Thunder in the first round of the playoffs.

title: Kerr on future: These jobs have 'expiration date'
content: Steve Kerr acknowledged his future with the Warriors was uncertain following a season-ending loss to the Suns, saying, "When the run ends, sometimes it's time for new blood and new ideas."

title: Magic, driven by 'urgency,' rip Hornets to advance
content: The Magic built a 35-point lead in the first half of their 121-90 rout of the Hornets and advanced to a first-round playoff matchup against the Pistons.

title: Source: Texans, Anderson agree to $150M deal
content: Texans and All-Pro defensive end Will Anderson Jr. have agreed to a historic three-year, $150 million extension to make him the highest-paid non-quarterback, a source told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Fever's Boston lands record $6.3M extension
content: Three-time WNBA All-Star Aliyah Boston has agreed to a four-year, $6.3 million contract extension with the Fever, her agent told ESPN.

title: Wesley sparks USWNT to 3-0 victory over Japan
content: Kennedy Wesley recorded her first international goal to lead the United States to a 3-0 friendly win over Japan on Friday night in Commerce City, Colorado.

title: Rockets list KD (knee) as questionable for Game 1
content: Kevin Durant sustained a right knee contusion during practice this week, but the Rockets are optimistic it will not be a significant issue during their first-round playoff series against the Lakers, a source told ESPN.

title: Retired stars Bird, Rapinoe announce separation
content: Retired sports stars Sue Bird and Megan Rapinoe announced on social media that, after a decade together, they are no longer in a relationship.

title: 🏈 NFL mini mock draft: Predicting top 10 picks
content: We asked NFL scouts to make draft picks for other teams, assigning one to each top-10 slot. Which prospects could go early?

content: Before we get to a weekend full of wrestling (Saturday and Sunday on ESPN Unlimited), our crew shares its thoughts on the must-see matches and more.

title: Inside the fallout of the Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel photos
content: How Dianna Russini and New England Patriots coach Mike Vrabel scrambled to respond to photos published in the New York Post.

title: Riddick's favorite players in the NFL draft class, from first-round locks to exciting sleepers
content: Louis Riddick picks his guys in the 2026 class, including some offensive playmakers and ball-hawking defensive backs. See who's on his 10-player list.

title: One year later: Who won the Jaguars-Browns draft-day trade for Travis Hunter?
content: The Browns and Jags swapped picks in 2025 so Jacksonville could draft Hunter. Now, Cleveland has an extra first-rounder in 2026.

content: How will Angel Reese fit the Dream? Will the Wings return to the playoffs? Can Caitlin Clark remain healthy?

content: Summer is here for franchises that missed the playoffs. What lies ahead for the Hornets, Clippers and more?

content: The USWNT just wrapped its window of three friendly games against Japan, but what did Emma Hayes learn ahead of the World Cup?

content: We've got potential title deciders in England and Germany, a grudge match in Portugal, and the Copa del Rey final: Julien Laurens previews the weekend and makes his predictions.

content: Arsenal's visit to Man City on Sunday will likely decide who wins the league. Here are the keys to the match.

title: Forget the 40: The true measure of Carnell Tate
content: Wide receiver Carnell Tate is confident, intelligent and ready to star in the NFL. His mother made sure of it.

title: Spring football games: Previewing Virginia Tech, Michigan, Miami and others
content: Spring football games are in full swing this weekend. Here's what to know ahead of Friday and Saturday.

title: Updated 2026 MLB draft rankings: Top 85 prospects and a 13-pick mock draft
content: The top of the 2026 MLB draft is coming into focus. Here's who your team could be targeting this summer.

title: 2026 NCAA gymnastics championships: Four teams advance out of semifinals
content: Florida, LSU, Oklahoma and Minnesota each advanced to Saturday's NCAA gymnastics final after a roller-coaster day of semifinals Thursday.

title: 'Friday' meets 'Thursday': Rams drop draft teaser inspired by Ice Cube's 1995 film
content: The short film inspired by the 1995 comedy "Friday" stars the sons of Ice Cube and Chris Tucker, joined by Rams players and celebrity cameos.

title: Biggest haters funeral: Chris Paul pokes fun at Clippers after play-in tournament loss
content: The LA Clippers saw their season end Wednesday, and an ex-Clipper made sure to rub salt in the wound.

title: Draft guide: Rankings, strategies and more
content: Everything you need to know before your fantasy women's basketball draft, including rankings, tips and news.

title: 10 NFL draft prospects to put on your fantasy radar -- and three others to monitor
content: With the NFL draft nearly upon us, it's time to think about how the top prospects will fare in fantasy.

title: Mason Miller tops all pitchers? CJ Abrams goes 30-100-30? Don't be surprised
content: Eric Karabell looks into the crystal ball to tell fantasy baseball managers what might come to pass in the not-too-distant future.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Liverpool eye Wharton amid Man United, Real Madrid interest
content: Liverpool face competition from Manchester United and Real Madrid to sign Crystal Palace midfielder Adam Wharton. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Gunfire in Hormuz: Indian-flagged tanker among ships targeted as Iran closes strait again
content: Mixed signals from Iran regarding the Strait of Hormuz have caused Indian and Greek crude tankers to turn back amid heightened tensions. Uncertainty over passage through the vital chokepoint deepened after conflicting reports from Tehran, with some merchant ships also reporting gunfire in the troubled waterway.

title: Allahabad HC stays FIR order against Rahul Gandhi in dual citizenship case
content: The Allahabad high court has temporarily halted its order for an FIR against Rahul Gandhi concerning alleged dual citizenship. The court emphasized the need to hear the accused before any decision. This follows an earlier directive to register a case based on claims of concealed British citizenship during company incorporation. The probe's transfer to a central agency was also permitted.

title: OpenAI exec Srinivas Narayanan leaves to spend time with ageing parents in India
content: Srinivas Narayanan, OpenAI's CTO for B2B applications, is departing the company next week after nearly three years. He plans to visit his parents in India before considering future professional endeavors. Narayanan, who previously served as VP of Engineering, shared his departure on LinkedIn, calling his tenure at the AI firm 'three incredible years'.

title: Tim David denies single, falls next ball as costly blunder triggers RCB slide
content: Tim David's refusal of a single proved costly as Royal Challengers Bengaluru faltered in their chase against Delhi Capitals. David's dismissal for 26 off 17 balls, after turning down a run, marked a significant shift in momentum. Earlier, a disciplined bowling effort from Delhi restricted RCB to 175 for 8, despite a strong start from Phil Salt.

title: Microsoft’s & Apple’s coding tools will be ‘dead soon,': Creator of Antrophic's AI coding tool
content: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, predicts traditional coding tools like VS Code and Xcode will soon be obsolete. He believes AI agents will soon handle entire development workflows, rendering current IDEs unnecessary. This shift will redefine software engineers into 'builders' who orchestrate AI, requiring adaptability and early experimentation from professionals.

title: $50,000M revenue blow: What led to half a billion barrels being erased during Iran war
content: A nearly 50-day Iran war has crippled global oil markets, removing over 500 million barrels and costing $50 billion. This unprecedented energy shock, impacting Gulf producers severely, could have long-lasting effects. While the Strait of Hormuz has reopened and a ceasefire is anticipated, a full recovery for energy supplies is expected to be a slow, multi-year process.

title: Women quota bill fails in Lok Sabha: Strategic move or poor planning?
content: For the first time since 2014, the Modi government failed to pass a bill in Parliament, a move analysts suggest was strategic. By forcing a vote on the women's reservation bill, linked to delimitation, the BJP aimed to record every MP's stance, creating a potent campaign tool. The government positioned itself as pro-women reformists, while blaming opposition parties for obstructing progress. This tactic seeks to shape the political narrative ahead of crucial elections, highlighting intent versus obstruction.

title: ‘Anti-Al’ attacker who tried to murder Sam Altman found ChatGPT ‘awesome’
content: Accused Molotov cocktail thrower Daniel Moreno-Gama, once a ChatGPT fan for 'cheating,' turned AI 'hater' after reading critics like Eliezer Yudkowsky. He joined peaceful activism groups like PauseAI, warning of extinction. Despite expressing fears of violence online, he was told to exhaust peaceful means first, ironically in a podcast interview.

title: ‘Samson will captain CSK’ - Ashwin makes bold claim on leadership shift
content: Ravichandran Ashwin sees Sanju Samson as a future captain for Chennai Super Kings. Ashwin believes Samson should forge his own path, not follow past leaders. The transition in CSK leadership has been ongoing. Samson has shown strong form recently. He will be a key player in upcoming matches.

title: Ahmed named head coach; Pakistan include 4 uncapped for B'desh Tests
content: Pakistan have named a 16-man squad for the Bangladesh Tests, led by Shan Masood, with Sarfaraz Ahmed as head coach. Sitting fifth in the WTC, they aim to improve against eighth-placed Bangladesh. Four uncapped players are included, alongside senior stars. A Karachi camp precedes the May 8 start, with matches in Dhaka and Sylhet.

title: Elderly Pakistani-origin couple found dead in Jaisalmer under suspicious circumstances
content: An elderly Pakistani-origin displaced couple was found dead under suspicious circumstances in their Jaisalmer home, with preliminary findings suggesting strangulation. The disappearance of the woman's silver anklets points towards a possible robbery motive. The couple, who had recently obtained Indian citizenship, lived alone and had no children.

title: DA hike: Govt approves 2% dearness allowance raise for central govt employees
content: Central government employees will see a 2% Dearness Allowance increase, approved by the Cabinet. This comes as employee bodies push for significant pay structure reforms under the proposed 8th Pay Commission, seeking a higher fitment factor and expanded family definitions for pay calculations.

title: Chasing likes, losing lives: How social media reels are fueling fatal risks among youth
content: Tragic incidents across India highlight the deadly risks of filming social media reels. Teenagers and young adults are losing their lives in crashes, drownings, and fatal falls while chasing viral fame. Experts and authorities urge greater awareness, parental supervision, and platform responsibility to curb this dangerous trend before more lives are lost.

title: PSL turns comedy show: Replacement player plays alongside player he replaced
content: A bizarre selection mix-up in the Pakistan Super League saw a replacement player feature alongside the one he was meant to replace. Quetta Gladiators mistakenly included both Dinesh Chandimal and Bevon Jacobs in their Playing XI due to a late illness to Tom Curran, causing social media outrage.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection: Can Ranveer Singh film dethrone 'Pushpa 2's domestic collection?
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' also known as 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has cemented its status as a cultural phenomenon, effectively redefining the "spy universe" in Indian cinema. As of April 18, 2026, the film is completing its first month in theaters, having shattered nearly every domestic record held by previous blockbusters. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the 229-minute espionage masterpiece is now setting its sights on an even more audacious target: the all-time Indian domestic box office record of Rs. 1,234.1 crore net currently held by Allu Arjun’s 'Pushpa 2: The Rule.'

title: 'Shouldn't be considered failure of govt ...': Rijiju on women's reservation bill not clearing LS
content: The government faced a setback as the women's reservation bill failed to pass in the Lok Sabha, requiring a two-thirds majority. Union Minister Kiren Rijiju blamed the Congress and opposition parties, calling it an anti-women stance and a significant blow to the nation. The opposition, while supporting reservation, opposed its linkage with delimitation and census.

title: 'Black day for Centre, they felt a shock for first time': Priyanka on delimitation bill defeat
content: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra hailed the defeat of the Delimitation Bill as a victory for democracy, accusing the Centre of a conspiracy to alter the federal structure. The bill, which proposed women's reservation linked to delimitation, failed to secure the required majority in the Lok Sabha. Opposition parties, while supporting women's reservation in principle, opposed its linkage with delimitation and census.

title: Not CSK or MI, RCB to become first ever IPL team to achieve rare milestone
content: The match at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium will be an afternoon game on the day the tournament completes 19 years.

title: PSL drama! Afridi loses temper, smashes bat in outburst - Watch
content: Lahore Qalandars captain Shaheen Shah Afridi displayed visible anger after being dismissed for a duck against Quetta Gladiators in the PSL 2026. His frustration, shown by slamming his bat, highlights the defending champions' inconsistent campaign and mounting pressure as they fight for playoff contention.

title: TCS Nashik case: No complaints through POSH channels, absconding female employee; what we know
content: A fact-finding committee has launched an inquiry into alleged sexual harassment and religious conversion at a Nashik TCS office. The panel, including a retired judge and IPS officer, will interview all stakeholders. Meanwhile, Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis called it a "very serious matter," investigating potential "corporate jihad." Police are searching for an accused employee.

title: Ali Asgar on drag typecasting: Comic says kids were bullied over ‘Dadi’
content: Iconic actor Ali Asgar, best known for his beloved 'Dadi' character, opened up about the emotional impact his drag performances had on his family. He shared that his children endured bullying at school because of his female roles, prompting him to turn down similar opportunities.

title: Shubman Gill dethrones Virat Kohli, takes big lead
content: Shubman Gill has taken the lead in the IPL 2026 Orange Cap race with 251 runs after a match-winning 86. Virat Kohli follows closely in second with 228 runs, while Heinrich Klaasen, Rajat Patidar, and Ishan Kishan round out the top five in a tightly contested batting battle.

title: ‘Ashok Kharat may be killed in encounter’: A warning before ‘astrologer’s’ aide dies in car crash
content: Close associate of jailed godman Ashok Kharat and his wife were killed in a road accident on the Samruddhi Expressway. Their 14-year-old son was seriously injured. The fatal crash has sparked suspicion from opposition leaders, though police are treating it as an accident.

title: What is so special about Iran’s uranium that US, China & Russia all want it?
content: Iran's enriched uranium stockpile has become a focal point in a global power contest, with the US, China, and Russia vying for influence. Tehran insists the material will not be handed over, despite US claims of an impending agreement for its recovery. This dispute highlights the complex political and strategic stakes surrounding Iran's nuclear program.

title: American Express executive found dead in Gurgaon; wife finds body inside flat
content: A 33-year-old American Express executive was found dead by suicide in his Gurgaon flat on Friday. His wife, who flew in from Mumbai after he stopped answering calls following a fight, discovered the scene. The couple, married four months ago, faced marital strain due to their differing work locations. Police are investigating the incident, noting the deceased was under stress.

title: Starlink’s India rollout faces FDI, security clearance roadblock: Report
content: Starlink's satellite internet rollout in India faces regulatory hurdles due to national security concerns. The government is cautiously reviewing its foreign direct investment proposal, with potential rejection if queries on cross-holding and network control are not addressed. Security clearance is pending, emphasizing the need to prevent potential misuse.

title: 'Bhooth Bangla' opens to strong Rs 23 crore worldwide debut; ends 'Dhurandhar 2' reign
content: Akshay Kumar's 'Bhooth Bangla' has stormed the box office, securing a massive Rs 23.90 crore worldwide debut on its opening day. This impressive performance has dethroned Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' after its four-week reign. The horror-comedy's strong opening positions it among Akshay Kumar's top post-pandemic releases.

title: JAC class 12 results likely soon as Jharkhand Board enters final evaluation phase
content: Jharkhand Academic Council is poised to unveil the Class 12 results for 2026 imminently, with the evaluation process nearing its end. Students can anticipate checking their scores online by late April or early May. This follows the recent declaration of Class 9 results, indicating a swift conclusion to the board's assessment cycle.

title: Don’t be afraid of artificial intelligence: CJI Surya Kant tells judicial officers
content: The CJI says the task before the judiciary is not to resist technological progress, but to integrate it in a manner that strengthens, rather than diminishes, the essence of justice

title: Explained: India tried to pass 33% reservation for women, it failed. Here’s why
content: Why did the Women’s Reservation Bill fail in India? This video explains the 33% reservation plan, the Lok Sabha vote, delimitation, and how political power and seat redistribution led to the Bill’s collapse.

title: NMC proposes to ease norms for establishing medical college, tighten PG education standards
content: The National Medical Commission in its proposed amendments to the “Registration of Medical Practitioners and Licence to Practice Medicine (Amendment), Regulations 2026” has also mooted the idea of introducing a single-State licence for Armed Forces Medical Services (AFMS) doctors to practice across the country

title: LPG crunch drains flavour from houseboat experience in Kerala’s backwaters
content: With cooking gas running dry, many houseboat operators have been forced to strip down their offerings to rides alone, while others have simply stopped taking bookings

title: Delhi Police detain two BJP MPs during protest march to Rahul Gandhi’s residence over Constitution Amendment Bill
content: BJP’s women workers held the protest march accusing the Opposition of ‘humiliating’ women of the country; officials said the Police used water cannons to disperse women protesters

title: Two Indian ships reverse course in Hormuz strait, says vessel tracker
content: Iran said earlier it was reimposing restrictions on the strait in response to a U.S. blockade on Iranian shipping and ports

title: Address representation gaps in Sixth Schedule areas: 3 Assam MPs
content: Say constitutional realignment can rectify historical imbalances and strengthen inclusion, and making population sole criterion for delimitation would be an injustice to northeast

title: Kerala has become ‘hub of love jihad’, alleges VHP
content: Vishwa Hindu Parishad national organising secretary Milind Parande says the organisation will submit a petition to the Union government seeking urgent intervention into the issue

title: Separatist leader Shabir Shah arrested in terror-related case, send to 10-day NIA remand
content: After hearing the submissions, the officials said, the court sent him to a 10-day NIA remand, allowing investigators to question him in the 1996 case.

title: Judiciary got only ‘peanuts’ from State funds in last three years: SC judge
content: Urges Chief Minister Siddaramaiah to direct officials to allocate at least 75-80% of court-collected revenue back for judiciary’s infrastructure

title: Opposition unity defeated Centre’s ‘conspiracy to alter federal structure and stay in power’, says Priyanka Gandhi
content: The Opposition is not opposed to women’s reservation, and would support the implementation of the Women’s Reservation Act, 2023 on the current strength of the Lok Sabha, the Congress general secretary says

title: Army canteen liquor smuggling racket busted in Assam
content: A joint team of Assam Police and Indian Army personnel arrested five people involved in diverting restricted supplies to unauthorised channels

title: Odisha to establish new advisory body to guide govt on policy matters
content: SITI Odisha will serve as a think tank of the State government—an apex policy research and strategic advisory body—aligned with the vision of , government says

title: BJP aimed to amend Constitution and scrap reservations: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy
content: Mr. Reddy  stated that the Congress would back a bill providing 33% reservation for women in the existing 543 Lok Sabha seats and in State Assemblies, if introduced immediately

title: Chief Minister N. Chandra Babu Naidu launches a national-level campaign against Opposition for defeating Constitution Amendment Bill
content: The Opposition parties defeated the bill citing adverse impact on small States, South and North Eastern States despite promise by Narendra Modi for the equal increase in the number of Lok Sabha seats: April 17 was a black day in Indian political history, said Mr. Naidu

title: CBI arrests former bank official, two others in ‘digital arrest’ case
content: The defrauded funds were routed to a bank account opened fraudulently in the name of a company to facilitate receipt of proceeds of cyber fraud, according to the CBI

title: Prime Minister, Vice-President pay tribute to Chandra Shekhar on birth anniversary
content: PM Modi recalled his personal interactions with the former Prime Minister and also urged young Indians to learn more about the leader’s work

title: Govt approves Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool with sovereign guarantee of ₹12,980 crore
content: The pool would cover all maritime risks like Hull and Machinery, Cargo, Protection and Indemnity and War risk

title: Voting for any party other than TMC means vote for BJP: Abhishek Banerjee
content: Addressing a gathering after a roadshow in Farakka, Abhishek Banerjee claimed that while the BJP was "breaking the Congress", the TMC was "thwarting the BJP's influence on the ground"

title: ADC Chairperson inspects Amaravati infra works
content: Reviewing the key junction near Venkatapalem village — considered the gateway to the capital — Chairperson instructed officials to take up bitumen (BT) works within the next week and make the stretch fully operational

title: Law and order remain sensitive but under control: Manipur CM
content: "The briefing by IGAR [South] Major General Gaurav Sharma at the secretariat today reaffirmed that the law and order situation in the State is sensitive but under control, with security forces responding to all disturbances with utmost care and restraint," says Chief Minister

title: Parliament adjourns sine die a day after Opposition defeats Constitution Amendment Bill
content: The session drew to a close with a symbolic first — all six stanzas of Vande Mataram were rendered in the Lok Sabha, following a government directive mandating the rendition of the full version at official events and public functions

title: PM Modi to address the nation tonight at 8:30 p.m.
content: The Prime Minister is expected to delve into the issue of the implementation of the women’s quota and the happenings in Parliament

title: Police arrest stand-up comedian Rafiq, released
content: Acting on a complaint from Jana Sena Party activists, police picked up Mr. Rafiq from his residence at Visakhapatnam

title: 40,000 RTI appeals disposed of in a year: Karnataka information commissioner
content: Karnataka Information Commission (Kalaburagi bench) and the Kalaburagi district administration organise workshop on RTI Act for district, taluk and gram panchayat PDOs

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Unions allege about 750 polling officials in Ernakulam ‘denied’ right to vote
content: Unions claim postal ballots were not ready when many of the polling officials reached their designated voter facilitation centres

title: IAF clears Pune runway for flights after frontline jet incident
content: The runway at Pune Airport, which was temporarily unavailable due to an incident involving an Indian Air Force aircraft, has now been restored and declared operational, the IAF said in a brief statement.

title: TVK not relying merely on road campaigns; massive people’s movement under way: Vijay
content: Though the responsibility for giving permission for poll campaigns rests with the ECI, the DMK government continues to exert pressure on the TVK candidates’ election campaign using some officials, Mr. Vijay claimed

title: Supreme Court cautions High Courts against growing tendency to publicly criticise subordinate judicial officers
content: “The High Court, being a court of record in the State, is expected to act as the guardian of the officers in the district judiciary,” the Bench said.

title: First batch of 431 Haj pilgrims leaves from J&K; CM sees them off at airport
content: Officials said all required arrangements for transportation, baggage handling and documentation have been put in place at the Haj House in Bemina to ensure a smooth departure process

title: Kerala BDS student death: Orthodontics department staff at Kannur college resign en masse amid protests
content: Seven staff members from the department step down together, marking a significant development in the ongoing unrest on campus; the management is yet to accept the resignations

title: J&K launches QR code-based identification system for all tourism stakeholders in Pahalgam
content: Move enables easy identification and verification of genuine and registered service providers, including pony-ride operators, hawkers, business establishments, as well as outside vendors.

title: Vice-President C.P. Radhakrishnan meets MPs after conclusion of Budget session
content: Leader of the House in the Rajya Sabha J.P. Nadda and LoP Mallikarjun Kharge were among those present

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: How a stool at polling booths makes a huge difference for these people
content: Members of Tamil Nadu Dwarfism Association have reiterated a set of demands they have always made when the hustings is round the corner

title: Kerala records all-time high daily power usage and peak demand as mercury rises
content: Kerala records a daily power consumption of 116.11 mu and peak demand of 6,013 MW on Friday (April 17)

title: 153 stolen mobile phones returned to owners in Bapatla
content: The distribution marked the fourth phase of a special mobile recovery drive conducted at the district police headquarters

title: Hyderabad receives light rain on April 18 afternoon, ahead of IPL match
content: The weather conditions are likely to be closely watched ahead of IPL match between Sunrisers Hyderabad and Chennai Super Kings  at Uppal stadium

title: Tribal man dies after consuming ‘arishtam’ in Kerala’s Wayanad; probe launched
content: Hospital authorities reportedly indicated that excessive alcohol consumption, along with the intake of ‘arishtam’, might have caused complications leading to his death

title: G20 satellite expected to be launched in 2027: ISRO chief Narayanan
content: Addressing scientists from DRDO, ISRO, and the Aeronautical Society of India at the Engineering Staff College of India, Dr. Narayanan also said India is the first country to succeed in placing 104 satellites, more than 100 satellites using a single rocket without any collision.

title: Civil Securities case: U.S. SEC, Adanis jointly seek extension; propose revised schedule
content: The SEC had sued Adani Group founder Gautam Adani and his nephew, Sagar, in November 2024, alleging they had misled investors by failing to disclose an alleged bribery scheme tied to Indian state officials

title: Telangana Govt pushes for free MMTS travel to boost use of public transport, offers to bear ticket fares
content: The high-level interaction primarily focused on resolving pending issues related to land acquisition, shifting of utilities and other bottlenecks hampering the construction of several proposed road overbridges and road underbridges (ROB/RUBs) within and outside the capital region

title: Kerala urges caution over contaminated ice as temperatures soar, steps up food safety checks
content: Kerala Health Minister says that in view of rising temperatures, Food Safety department has intensified special inspections focusing on drinking water sources, juice shops, and beverage manufacturing units

title: Delimitation debate: US model of population vs equality in two Houses
content: the united states has been capped at 435 seats since 1929 . after every decennial census those seats are redistributed among the states according to population . states such as California, Florida and Texas have gained representation .

content: more than 60% of the players in the SC Next 300 remain uncommitted . the summer months will be pivotal in shaping the rankings . eight teams that finished with a top-10 class for the 2026 cycle have picked up where they left off with classes again near the top nationally .

title: 80-year-old Sikh, inspired by Fauja Singh, to run London Marathon for first time
content: the 80-year-old man is running the London Marathon for the first time on April 26 . he is inspired by Fauja Singh who encouraged him to take up walking . Baldev Singh Bains is doing the marathon to raise money for the fauja clubhouse .

title: 19 going on 35: How Konnor Griffin's maturity is making an impression on Pirates
content: the 19-year-old Pittsburgh Pirates rookie gave Konnor Griffin a franchise-record contract extension . he struck out on five pitches, three of which were over 100 mph . "now, I've done it," an analyst says .

title: How the Stanley Cup will be won: Wyshynski's predictions for the entire bracket
content: the 2026 Stanley Cup playoffs are here, and they're weird . the Buffalo Sabres , who last made the playoffs when "Fast Five" was in theaters, are here . but that's why the tournament is the greatest postseason in sports: the unpredictability .

title: Who's winning the Stanley Cup? All the intel you need on the 16 playoff teams
content: the Eastern Conference bracket was set earlier this week, including the Carolina Hurricanes and Buffalo Sabres as division champions . the Edmonton Oilers and Anaheim Ducks will face off as the Nos. 2 and 3 seeds, while the Los Angeles Kings will play the Avalanche as the second wild card .

title: Before Boiler Blast, Chattisgarh's Worst Industrial Disaster Took Place At Vedanta Plant
content: Since the formation of the Chhattisgarh state, numerous industrial accidents have occurred in succession, a majority of these at Vedanta facilities.

title: Iran Navy "Stands Ready" To Defeat US, Warns Mojtaba Khamenei
content: In a statement carried on his Telegram channel, Khamenei, who has not been seen in public since before the start of the war, said Iran's "brave navy stands ready to make the enemies taste the bitterness of new defeats".

title: Centre Committed To Bringing 33% Women's Reservation Before 2029: Sources
content: The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, had proposed 33 per cent reservation for women in parliament and assemblies along with an increase in the strength of the Lok Sabha

title: "Feudal Mindset": Smriti Irani Slams Congress Over Women's Reservation Bill
content: Senior BJP leaders on Saturday launched a scathing attack on the Congress, accusing it of "betraying" women and celebrating the defeat of their political aspirations

title: Adopted 'Playboy' Persona: Maharashtra Man Accused Of Exploiting Minors
content: One police official and two constables have been suspended after videos showed them celebrating Ayaz's birthday.

title: Mentally Challenged Woman Rescued From Rooftop, Kept Requesting For Food
content: Seema Soni, 40, was found in a deeply disturbing state. Her body was reduced to a skeletal frame. She lay exposed to the harsh sun, without clothes or food.

title: Naveen Jindal Faces Backlash For Defending Vedanta Boss Over Chhattisgarh Blast
content: In an incident where nearly two dozen lives have been lost, what came as a shock was Naveen Jindal's post questioning the Vedanta boss's name being added to the FIR.

title: Opinion: Opinion: Seats In South And West That Hold Key To Tamil Nadu
content: The DMK had a full sweep in Chennai and peaked in the north and central districts and the south in 2021

title: BJP's Women's Bill March To Rahul Gandhi's House, And A 'Dhokebaaz' Poster
content: "Half of the country's population will never forgive these villains who stopped the 33 per cent reservation for women power," the BJP said in a post on X

title: 'US Cannot Impose Their Will And Block Hormuz Strait': Iran Minister
content: "Americans cannot impose their will to do a siege over Iran while Iran, with good intention, is trying to facilitate safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz," said Saeed Khatibzadeh.

title: Karnataka Reports First Bird Flu Case Of 2026, Thousands Of Chickens Culled
content: Karnataka has reported its first case of avian influenza (H5N1) this year after the virus was detected in a poultry farm in Muthur village near Hesaraghatta on the outskirts of Bengaluru.

title: Cheetahs Back In Karnataka After 70 Years, 4 Brought In From South Africa
content: These felines are being brought in as part of the Project Cheetah, a landmark wildlife initiative launched by the central government in 2022.

title: Bengaluru Power Bills To Rise As Supply Company Gets Nod To Recover Deficit
content: Electricity consumers in Bengaluru are set to face a moderate increase in power bills after the Karnataka Electricity Regulatory Commission (KERC) approved a revenue deficit recovery plan for Bangalore Electricity Supply Company

title: Johnson vs Johnson: Dramatic Family Battle In $18 Trillion Empire
content: Abigail Johnson, daughter of then-chairman Edward “Ned” Johnson III, had been leading Fidelity’s flagship mutual-funds division.

title: 'Opposition Made A Mistake, Will Face Consequences': PM Modi On Women's Quota Bill Failing Parliament Test
content: Opposition must face the consequences and answer the women, sources have said, quoting PM Modi during a cabinet meeting.

title: Iran Shuts Hormuz Again, Accuses US Of Violating Deal To Reopen It
content: Iran Reimposes Restrictions On Strait Of Hormuz Amid Middle East War, Accuses US Of Violating Deal To Reopen It

title: "7 Claims In 1 Hour, All False": Iran After Trump's 'Hormuz Blockade' Post
content: Ghalibaf, in a post on X, asserted that the passage through the Strait of Hormuz will be conducted based on the "designated route" and with "Iranian authorisation".
